snippet.title: Women in Amateur Radio: POTA, SOTA and the Future of Ham Radio
snippet.description:
In this special International Women’s Day episode, we shine a spotlight on the incredible women helping shape the future of ham radio. From Parks on the Air (POTA) activations to portable operating, community building, and technical expertise, these operators represent some of the most passionate and knowledgeable voices in the hobby today.

We’re joined by an outstanding panel of YLs who are making waves across amateur radio. Each guest brings their own unique experience, operating style, and perspective on what makes this hobby so powerful and welcoming.

Whether you're new to ham radio or a long-time operator, this conversation highlights the passion, skill, and leadership women bring to the amateur radio community.

This episode also ties into the upcoming POTA Party event, celebrating operators getting out into parks, activating stations, and making contacts around the world.

📡 Guests on this episode include:

Shannon KC1OHT

Shannon K1LY

Elle-Marie KJ5LXP

Sandy KE8UTX

Julie KJ4ULZ

Caitlyn K8FAE

Amy AG7GP

Paula K9IR

We’ll talk about:

• Getting started in ham radio
• Operating portable with POTA and SOTA
• Building community in the hobby
• Advice for new operators
• Why amateur radio continues to grow and evolve

Join us for a fun and inspiring conversation celebrating women in amateur radio and the operators helping keep the bands alive.

- Shannon (KC1OHT) - https://www.youtube.com/@shannonkc1oht

snippet.title: 📡 INTERCETTO LE COMUNICAZIONI DEI PILOTI - COSTRUISCO UN'ANTENNA VHF FAI DA TE
snippet.description:
In questo video mostro passo dopo passo la costruzione fai da te di un’antenna in banda VHF dedicata ai radioamatori, utilizzando materiali facilmente reperibili e soluzioni semplici ma efficaci. 

Dalla progettazione alle misure, fino al montaggio e alla taratura finale, spiego ogni fase del processo per ottenere buone prestazioni di ricezione. 

Nella seconda parte del video metto alla prova l’antenna con alcune sessioni di ascolto in banda aeronautica, condividendo esempi di comunicazioni tra piloti e torri di controllo, nel rispetto delle normative vigenti. 

Un progetto pratico pensato per chi vuole sperimentare, migliorare il proprio impianto e approfondire il mondo delle VHF.

snippet.title: Kafa Karışıklığına Emniyet Genel Müdürlüğünden Açıklama Geldi!
snippet.description: Kamuoyunda sıkça tartışılan ve “APP plaka” olarak bilinen standart dışı araç plakalarıyla ilgili yapılan açıklamada, araç plakalarının belirli ölçü ve standartlara uygun olması gerektiği hatırlatıldı. Yönetmelikte belirtilen ölçü ve özelliklere uymayan, yani standart dışı plaka kullanan sürücülere 4 bin TL idari para cezası uygulanacak. Fakat plakanın yetkili kurum tarafından basılmamış ve mühürsüz olması durumunda ise cezaların çok daha ağır olduğu vurgulandı. Bu şekilde mühürsüz ve yetkisiz basılmış APP plaka kullanan sürücülere 140 bin TL para cezası kesilebileceği, sürücünün ehliyetine 30 gün el konulabileceği ve aracın da 30 gün trafikten men edilebileceği ifade edildi.

snippet.title: Bachcha Raat Me Nahi Sota? Daadi Ke 5 Asardar Nuskhe | Baby Sleep Tips #newborncare #babysleep
snippet.description:
👵 Daadi Maa Ke Asardar Nuskhe

Kya aapka bachcha bhi raat me jaldi nahi sota?
Kai baar maa poori raat jagti rehti hai aur samajh nahi aata ki bachche ko kaise sulaye.

Is video me Daadi ke bataye 5 simple aur pyaar bhare nuskhe hain jo bachche ko aaram se sulane me madad kar sakte hain. 👶❤️

✔️ Sone ka routine banana
✔️ Sulate waqt lori ya halki humming
✔️ Pet halka massage
✔️ Room ki light soft rakhna
✔️ Baby ko comfort feel karwana

Chhoti chhoti baatein hi bachche ki neend ko aasan bana deti hain.

Har maa ka apna experience hota hai.
👇 Comment me zaroor batayein — aapka bachcha raat me kitne baje sota hai?

Agar video useful lage to
❤️ Like karein
📤 Share karein
🔔 Channel ko Subscribe karein

Taaki aapko milte rahein Daadi Maa ke tested parenting nuskhe.

snippet.title: Newborn Baby 20-21 Ghante Kyun Sota Hai? Safe Sleeping Tips #babycare #newbornsleep #shorts #viral
snippet.description:
👉 Kya aapka newborn 20-21 ghante sota hai? Ye normal hai! 😴
Janam ke baad baby zyada sota hai kyunki uska brain aur body fast develop ho raha hota hai. 🧠
Burp karne ke baad baby ko karwat lekar sulaiye, seedha bhi sula sakte hain aur thoda tummy time dena bhi zaruri hai. 🤱
Sleeping position change karte rehna baby ki safety aur comfort ke liye bahut important hai.

🌿 Healthy sleep habits follow karein aur baby ko safe rakhein!

snippet.title: Dadi Nay Banai Palm Leaves Say Jeep 😱 | Pota Khush Hogaya ❤️
snippet.description:
एक अमीर बच्चा अपने महंगे खिलौने वाली कार को एक गरीब बच्चे को देखने भी नहीं देता। जब गरीब बच्चा कार के पास जाता है, तो अमीर बच्चा उसे धक्का देकर नीचे गिरा देता है।

गरीब बच्चा दुखी होकर घर जाता है और अपनी दादी को सारी बात बताता है। उसकी दादी उसे खुश करने के लिए खजूर के पत्तों (Palm Leaves) से एक शानदार जीप बनाना शुरू कर देती है।

धीरे-धीरे दादी खजूर के पत्तों से पूरी जीप तैयार कर देती है – जिसमें पहिए, सीट, स्टेयरिंग सब कुछ पत्तों से बना होता है। आखिर में गरीब बच्चा उस जीप में बैठकर बहुत खुश हो जाता है।

यह एक इमोशनल और प्रेरणादायक कहानी है जो दिखाती है कि प्यार और मेहनत से कुछ भी बनाया जा सकता है।

अगर आपको यह कहानी पसंद आए तो वीडियो को लाइक करें और चैनल को सब्सक्राइब जरूर करें।

Georgia Electrician 2026 Licensing Guide | Requirements, Exams & NEC Updates
Are you ready to become a licensed electrical professional in Georgia in 2026? This video breaks down everything you need to know — from the state licensing requirements and exam structure to the latest NEC code updates that will impact your prep and career. ⚡

In this video you’ll learn:
✔ What the Georgia Electrical Contractor license is and who needs it
✔ The Class I vs. Class II license differences
✔ Experience & age requirements to qualify
✔ How to apply and get approved to sit for the exam
✔ What the licensing exam covers and passing score expectations
✔ Important 2026 NEC (National Electrical Code) updates adopted in Georgia
✔ Tips to prepare for the open-book exam and reference materials
✔ Renewal & continuing education basics

Georgia requires electrical contractors to be licensed by the Georgia State Construction Industry Licensing Board, Division of Electrical Contractors — journeymen and apprentices work under supervision but contracting work over certain limits must be licensed.

📊 Exam Details

Administered through PSI

Must score at least 70% to pass

Covers laws, codes, regulations, electrical theory, and NEC-based technical topics

Reference books like the NEC, OSHA CFR 1926, and Georgia exam references are used during the test.

2026 NEC Updates in Georgia
Effective Jan 1, 2026, Georgia adopted the 2026 Georgia Amendments to the 2023 NEC, aligning state electrical codes with the latest safety standards. This will directly impact electrical work and exams moving forward, so understanding these changes is critical for success.

snippet.title: NEVER EVER Miss A POTA PARK Ever Again!
snippet.description:
Are you tired of manually checking POTAPlus to see if a spotted park is one you actually need? In this video, I’ll show you a game-changing workflow to automate your "New One" notifications using HamAlert.

Stop staring at the map and start hunting with precision! Whether you're chasing "Worked All Parks" or just looking to fill those empty spots in your log, this method ensures your phone (or your PC) pings the second a park you haven't worked hits the air.

In this video, we cover:

📥 How to export your unworked park list directly from the POTA website.
📊 Cleaning your CSV file to get a clean list of "Needed" references (US-XXXX, CA-XXXX).
⚙️ Setting up a custom POTA trigger in HamAlert.
🖥️ A look at the OE3IDE Windows Cluster Client for desktop notifications.

Special thanks to Pierre VE2GT for sharing this brilliant tip within our Ham Radio Friends group!

🔗 Links Mentioned:
POTA Park List: https://pota.app/#/parklist
HamAlert: https://hamalert.org/
OE3IDE HamAlert Cluster Client: https://www.oe3ide.com/hamalert-cluster-client/

📻 Join the Community:
Blog: Out N' Aboot with VE9CF at https://www.outnaboot.ca/

73 de VE9CF

snippet.title: The First Timers GUIDE To Parks On The Air
snippet.description:
New to Parks On The Air? Welcome to the wonderful world of portable amateur radio! In this comprehensive beginner's guide, I'll walk you through everything you need to know to get started as a first-time POTA participant.

Parks On The Air (POTA) is a wildly popular amateur radio activity that combines the hobby we love with the great outdoors. Whether you're interested in being an activator (operating from a park) or a hunter/chaser (making contacts with activators from your home station), this video will give you the foundation you need to jump right in.

What We'll Cover:

We'll start by exploring the main pota.app website, which is your central hub for all things POTA. I'll show you how to navigate the interface, create your account, and familiarize yourself with the essential features you'll be using regularly.

Next, we'll dive into the spotting system so you can see in real-time who's currently out activating parks around the world. I'll explain how to read the spots, understand the different modes and bands being used, and how you can jump on the air to make those valuable park-to-park contacts.

We'll also take a look at the basic awards program that makes POTA so addictive! From your first activator and hunter awards to the various achievement levels, I'll explain how the awards system works and what you can work toward as you grow in the program.

Finally, I'll give you a complete tour of the website platform, showing you where to find park information, how to look up reference numbers, view statistics, and access all the tools you'll need for a successful POTA experience.

Whether you're a brand-new ham looking for your first on-air activity or an experienced operator curious about portable operations, this guide will help you get started with confidence. POTA is one of the most beginner-friendly and rewarding activities in amateur radio, and I'm excited to help you begin your journey!

Don't forget to like, subscribe, and leave your questions in the comments below. I love hearing from the POTA community and helping newcomers get on the air!

73 and happy hunting!

#POTA #HamRadio #AmateurRadio #ParksOnTheAir #PortableOperations #HamRadioForBeginners #POTAActivation #POTAHunter #GetOnTheAir

Next, we'll dive into the spotting system so you can see in real-time who's currently out activating parks around the world. I'll explain how to read the spots, understand the different modes and bands being used, and how you can jump on the air to make those valuable park-to-park contacts.

We'll also take a look at the basic awards program that makes POTA so addictive! From your first activator and hunter awards to the various achievement levels, I'll explain how the awards system works and what you can work toward as you grow in the program.

Finally, I'll give you a complete tour of the website platform, showing you where to find park information, how to look up reference numbers, view statistics, and access all the tools you'll need for a successful POTA experience.

Whether you're a brand-new ham looking for your first on-air activity or an experienced operator curious about portable operations, this guide will help you get started with confidence. POTA is one of the most beginner-friendly and rewarding activities in amateur radio, and I'm excited to help you begin your journey!

Don't forget to like, subscribe, and leave your questions in the comments below. I love hearing from the POTA community and helping newcomers get on the air!

73 and happy hunting!

#POTA #HamRadio #AmateurRadio #ParksOnTheAir #PortableOperations #HamRadioForBeginners #POTAActivation #POTAHunter #GetOnTheAir

snippet.title: How to Set Up Ham Radio Mobile Comms In Your Car/Truck - The ULTIMATE Guide!
snippet.description:
Want to turn your car or truck into a fully capable mobile ham radio station for both local repeater use and long-distance HF contacts? In this complete step-by-step guide, James Cribbs (N0WRL) walks you through everything you need — radio selection, antennas, grounding, coax routing, power setup, and a live Parks on the Air activation to prove it works. You’ll learn how to build a powerful, reliable mobile station for under $1,000 using practical, affordable gear — as well as the top of the line Icom IC-7100 “shack in a box.” Whether you’re new to mobile installs or looking to upgrade your setup, this video covers the critical details most hams overlook — especially grounding and antenna performance.

This video is a sample of our POTA Course! 

The complete version of the video in the POTA course contains links to all the materials used, step by step guides, and a printable PDF.

👉 Sign up here: https://hamradioprep.com/pota

Chapters
00:00 Introduction – Building a Mobile Ham Station
01:46 VHF/UHF vs HF Explained (Local vs Long Distance)
02:49 Best All-Band Mobile Radio (Icom IC-7100)
04:30 HF-Only & VHF/UHF Radio Alternatives
05:24 Choosing the Right Mobile Antenna (Hamsticks & More)
07:47 Why Grounding Matters (Critical Step Most Hams Miss)
10:15 Installing the Antenna Mount (Metal-to-Metal Contact)
12:10 Choosing the Right Coax (LMR240 vs RG8X vs RG58)
14:30 Routing Coax Into the Vehicle
16:45 Checking SWR with an Antenna Analyzer
18:17 Powering Your Mobile Station (LiFePO4 vs Car Battery)
21:32 Live POTA Activation from the Vehicle
27:45 Performance Results & Contact Distance
31:20 Final Thoughts, Improvements & Optimization Tips

🎓 Need your license? Visit https://HamRadioPrep.com to get started.
🌎 Track contacts and log activations with https://WorldRadioLeague.com

snippet.title: U.S. Treasury License Allows Russian Oil Already at Sea to Reach India Until April 4, 2026
snippet.description:
The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control issues General License 133, a quiet but significant move allowing Russian-origin crude oil and petroleum products already loaded onto vessels on or before March 5, 2026 to be legally sold, delivered, and offloaded until April 4, 2026. This license provides Indian refineries with a legal pathway to receive millions of barrels of Russian crude currently stranded in floating storage due to sanctions.

#RussianOil #IndiaOilImports #USSanctions #OFAC #GeneralLicense133 #GlobalOilMarket #EnergyPolitics #RussiaIndiaTrade #OilNews #BreakingNews #indiatodayglobal 

The exemption applies only if shipments are delivered to Indian ports and purchased by entities registered under Indian law. It does not broadly lift sanctions on Russia or allow transactions with other restricted countries such as Iran, but it provides Moscow with temporary financial relief. India continues to play a critical role as a major buyer of Russian oil, ensuring continued revenues for Moscow amid Western market restrictions.

snippet.description:
¡Hoy estrenamos nuestro nuevo programa de CanalYA! Planeta Mar.

La pota peruana está en peligro por culpa de los chinos, pero hay una manera de frenarlos.

Aunque el Perú lideraba su captura, China ha superado al país utilizando buques industriales subsidiados que operan ilegalmente y han sido señalados por graves abusos a los derechos humanos.

ADEMÁS... la construcción ilegal de barcos en Perú y la resistencia al monitoreo satelital agravan la presión sobre la pota, dificultando su sostenibilidad. 

Para frenar el ingreso irregular a las 200 millas, el Estado peruano ha endurecido las reglas de vigilancia, logrando que muchos barcos chinos se desplacen hacia otros países como Chile.

LO QUE SE VIENE...ante la reunión de la OROP en marzo de 2026, el Perú propone una "zona buffer" de 20 millas para proteger el ecosistema y asegurar que la actividad en aguas internacionales no agote el mar peruano

snippet.title: Contesting Introduction – with N6KI
snippet.description:
Are you curious about ham radio contesting but not sure where to start? In this beginner-friendly presentation, Dennis Vernacchia (N6KI) shares decades of experience to explain how amateur radio contesting works and why so many operators enjoy it.

Dennis has been a licensed amateur radio operator since 1963 and an active contester since 1988. In this presentation, he walks through the fundamentals of contesting, including the equipment you need, popular logging software, operating strategies, and how beginners can get on the air and start making contacts.

This video is ideal for new ham radio operators, technicians looking to upgrade their skills, and anyone interested in competitive amateur radio operating.

To improve the viewing experience, this version of the recording includes Dennis's presentation slides inserted directly into the video, making the information easier to read than in the original live stream.

Topics covered in this presentation include:

• What ham radio contesting is and why operators participate
• Basic equipment needed to get started contesting
• Popular ham radio logging software used during contests
• Beginner operating strategies and techniques
• Tips for making contacts and improving your score
• Why contesting is a great way to build operating skills

Whether you're new to amateur radio or looking to explore a new aspect of the hobby, this presentation will help you understand how ham radio contests work and how you can participate.

This presentation was recorded for the San Diego Repeater Association (SANDRA) and is brought to you by KE6VDW.

Support your local amateur radio community by joining or donating to SANDRA:
http://sandra.radio

snippet.title: Passed My Technician Exam with 100% in Just Two Weeks | Bill's Story
snippet.description:
Bill KO6JWW had never held a ham radio license before. Like many beginners, he started with the goal of earning his Technician license and searched for a structured way to study.

After enrolling in the Ham Radio Prep Technician course, Bill found the lessons both easy to follow and comprehensive, giving him a strong understanding of the material even faster than he expected.

One thing that surprised him was how much background knowledge he already had that applied to ham radio concepts. The course helped connect those ideas and organize them in a way that made studying straightforward.

Within about one week, Bill already felt ready to take the exam. He scheduled his test for the following week and continued practicing with the built-in practice exams while he waited.

By the time test day arrived, he felt confident — and it paid off.

Bill passed his Technician exam with a perfect score of 100% on the first try.

Now that he has his Technician license, Bill plans to continue his journey by studying for the General license next.

snippet.title: Is This My New Favorite?
snippet.description:
In this video I review the Digitenna multi-band portable antenna from DigiRig, a compact HF antenna designed for amateur radio operators who want reliable performance in the field. The version I’m testing covers 10m, 15m, 20m, and 40m bands, making it a versatile option for portable ham radio, POTA, SOTA, field operations, and emergency communications.
I walk through the build quality, setup, SWR performance, and on-air usability of the Digitenna. Right away the construction quality stands out—everything from the connectors to the materials feels durable and well-engineered for portable use. If you're looking for a lightweight portable HF antenna that packs easily but still performs well, this antenna definitely deserves a look.

snippet.title: Women in Amateur Radio: POTA, SOTA and the Future of Ham Radio
snippet.description:
In this special International Women’s Day episode, we shine a spotlight on the incredible women helping shape the future of ham radio. From Parks on the Air (POTA) activations to portable operating, community building, and technical expertise, these operators represent some of the most passionate and knowledgeable voices in the hobby today.

We’re joined by an outstanding panel of YLs who are making waves across amateur radio. Each guest brings their own unique experience, operating style, and perspective on what makes this hobby so powerful and welcoming.

Whether you're new to ham radio or a long-time operator, this conversation highlights the passion, skill, and leadership women bring to the amateur radio community.

This episode also ties into the upcoming POTA Party event, celebrating operators getting out into parks, activating stations, and making contacts around the world.

📡 Guests on this episode include:

Shannon KC1OHT

Shannon K1LY

Elle-Marie KJ5LXP

Sandy KE8UTX

Julie KJ4ULZ

Caitlyn K8FAE

Amy AG7GP

Paula K9IR

We’ll talk about:

• Getting started in ham radio
• Operating portable with POTA and SOTA
• Building community in the hobby
• Advice for new operators
• Why amateur radio continues to grow and evolve

Join us for a fun and inspiring conversation celebrating women in amateur radio and the operators helping keep the bands alive.

snippet.title: U.S. Quietly Allows Russian Oil Shipments to India, Until April, Under New Treasury License
snippet.description:
A quiet but significant move by the United States may reshape short-term global oil flows. The U.S. Treasury's Office of Foreign Assets Control issues General License 133, allowing the sale, delivery, and offloading of Russian-origin crude oil and petroleum products that were already loaded onto vessels before March 5, 2026, until April 4, 2026.

#RussianOil #IndiaOilImports #USSanctions #GlobalOilMarket #EnergyPolitics #RussiaIndiaTrade #BreakingNews #OilMarket #Geopolitics #EnergyNews #indiatodayglobal 

The authorization comes with a crucial condition. The shipments must be delivered to ports in India and purchased by entities organized under Indian law. This effectively allows Indian refineries to receive millions of barrels of Russian crude currently stuck in floating storage due to sanctions restrictions. While the decision does not remove sanctions on Russia entirely, it creates a narrow exemption for shipments already in transit. The move ensures that existing supply chains can be completed while maintaining broader sanctions restrictions. For Russia, the exemption helps keep oil revenues flowing. For India, it secures continued access to discounted crude supplies that have become a key part of its energy strategy.

India Today Global is designed to cater to the Indian diaspora in the US while also appealing to international viewers interested in South Asian affairs and perspectives on global news.

snippet.title: N1MM Logger+ Confused? This Beginner's Guide Makes It EASY
snippet.description:
Are you a new ham radio operator looking to jump into the exciting world of contesting? With the ARRL International DX Contest (SSB) coming up this weekend on March 7-8, 2026, there has never been a better time to get your station ready and make some worldwide contacts! 🌎📻

In this video, we cover the absolute basics of getting started with N1MM Logger+ — the most popular, free contest logging software used by amateur radio operators around the globe. Whether you're just aiming to make a few casual contacts or want to seriously compete, setting up your logging software correctly is step number one.

If you've been intimidated by contesting software in the past, don't worry—we keep it simple and beginner-friendly so you can get on the air, bust some pileups, and have fun.

🔗 Helpful Links & Resources:

• Download N1MM Logger+: https://n1mmwp.hamdocs.com/
• ARRL International DX Contest Rules: http://www.arrl.org/arrl-dx

👍 Join the Community!

If this video helped you get your station contest-ready, please hit the LIKE button and SUBSCRIBE for more beginner-friendly ham radio tutorials.

👇 Question of the Day: Are you planning to operate in the ARRL DX contest this weekend? Which bands are you hoping to work with? Let me know in the comments below! 73!

#HamRadio #AmateurRadio #N1MMLogger #Contesting #ARRLDX #HamRadioBeginner #QSO #Radiosport #HamRadioTube

73 de Stuart

snippet.title: How to Get Your General Contractor License With NO Experience (Most People Don’t Know This) #shorts
snippet.description:
Can you really get your General Contractor License with NO experience?

A lot of people will tell you it’s impossible.

They’ll say:
❌ You need years of experience
❌ You need someone to sign off
❌ You need to work under another contractor first

But the truth is…

In many states, the biggest barrier isn’t experience — it’s passing the exam.

And that’s what most people are actually afraid of.

I help students pass their General Contractor License Exam all the time — including people with zero construction experience.

Here’s the reality:

✔ Some states are strict (like Florida and California)
✔ Some states allow alternative paths
✔ Sometimes it’s about who signs off on your application
✔ But first… you must pass the exam

You can’t do the work until you pass the test.

So stop listening to people telling you it’s impossible.

If you’re ready to start studying today and finally get your contractor license, we built a platform specifically for you.

👉 Start studying now:
https://studybuddypro.app

The only thing standing between you and your contractor license…
is the exam.

snippet.title: This WEEKEND Promises To Be HUGE For POTA Activators (Trust Me)
snippet.description:
🎉 Calling all Parks On The Air (POTA) operators! This weekend is hands-down the BEST weekend of the year for working DX from the great outdoors—and here’s why…

🌍 It’s the annual ARRL DX Contest, one of the biggest events on the amateur radio calendar! Operators from all over the world will be lighting up the bands, making this the ultimate opportunity to log some incredible DX from your favourite park.

👉 Instead of worrying about crowded bands or struggling to find a clear spot to call CQ POTA, embrace the excitement! Spin the dial, hunt for those rare stations, and rack up QSO after QSO. Whether you’re a seasoned activator or new to POTA, this is your chance to make contacts you might not hear any other time of year.

Why this is NOT the weekend to sit it out or complain about busy bands—get out there and make the most of it!

📻 Grab your rig, pack your gear, head to a park, and let’s make some radio magic happen. Don’t miss this golden opportunity—see you on the air!

#POTA #ParksOnTheAir #ARRLDXContest #AmateurRadio #HamRadio #DX #FieldRadio #ContestTips

👇 Drop a comment if you’ll be activating this weekend and share your best DX catches!

snippet.title: Best HF Radios Ever Made? | FTDX9000MP & IC-7850
snippet.description:
Two legendary HF flagships… but which would you choose?

This week’s Something for the Weekend features two of the most desirable radios ever built - the Yaesu FTDX9000MP and the Icom IC-7850 Limited Edition. Both were five-figure radios when new, both are incredibly rare today, and both represent the peak of traditional high-end amateur radio engineering.

Which flagship would you choose for your shack?
